set.body:
Problem

Gale can allocate a zero-filled DMatBuilder, but cannot initialize one from an existing possibly strided DMat in one owned copy. Public bulk export is valuesRowMajor: Seq[Double], which boxes; efficient primitive row-major copies remain package-private. Downstream code therefore reimplements row and column selection, primitive copying, copy-modify-rebuild transforms, diagonal regularization, and average symmetrization.

Scope

Add a cohesive owned transformation and indexing surface:
- DMatBuilder.from(matrix) and/or matrix.toBuilder, performing exactly one owned logical row-major copy from contiguous, transposed, or strided input.
- Contiguous matrix slice APIs that return strided views and preserve view semantics.
- gatherRows(indices) and gatherColumns(indices), returning owned matrices and preserving repeated-index order.
- DVec.gather(indices), returning an owned vector.
- addToDiagonal(amount), returning an owned matrix without changing off-diagonal values.
- Explicit average symmetrization with semantics B(i,j) = (A(i,j) + A(j,i)) / 2 and square-shape validation.
- Allocation-free foreachRowMajor and/or copyRowMajorTo, plus vector equivalents, using primitive destinations or callbacks without exposing adoptable backing storage.

Acceptance

- Public construction and traversal preserve storage encapsulation: callers cannot adopt or retain an aliased Gale backing array.
- Builder initialization makes one owned copy, result transfers builder ownership, and closed-after-result behavior remains intact.
- Slice view versus gather copy ownership is explicit in Scaladoc and tests.
- JVM and Scala.js tests cover row-major, transposed, and strided inputs; repeated gather indices; invalid indices and ranges; zero-row, zero-column, and empty-vector cases; diagonal and symmetrization semantics.
- Platform-appropriate allocation/boxing tests or receipts demonstrate single-copy builder creation and allocation-free traversal on both JVM and Scala.js.
- Existing DMat, DVec, Matrix, and Vec entry points expose the new operations consistently without introducing domain-specific policy.
set.title: Owned dense transformation, gather, and primitive traversal APIs
